title: Update `ROADMAP.md`
body:
The file [ROADMAP.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/ROADMAP.md) has not seen an update within the last months and ends in Q3 2025 with Podman version 5.6.

Q1 2026 will be over in a few weeks so the roadmap should at least include Q2 with Podman version 6.0.

comments.body:
My honest opinion this file should be removed. It serves no purpose with one liners that do not link anywhere for people to follow progress and/or leave feedback.

If there should be a public roadmap it should have public issues attached (ideally Github issues and not the Red Hat Jira we use for internal team planning)
Since @baude is less involved with the project, maybe I should take this one over? Or @ddarrah
I agree with @Luap99, it is just a duplication (or at least it should be) of what happens in the public github project

A weak +1: a roadmap file, or GNU-style ChageLog, brings to mind the time when cathedral-style Free Software was distributed by mailing around tapes.

But then again, with >900 issues in this repo, that doesn’t make it very easy to find out what is truly going on at any given month.
From a user point of view it's interesting to see which big changes are expected to come, but maybe it's not necessary. In any case it's better to have an updated roadmap or no roadmap at all.

Today I found out how the `flatcar` team handles its roadmap: https://github.com/orgs/flatcar/projects/7/views/9

They are using the Github Projects boards and add labels like `kind/roadmap` to their issues.

To be clear when I say the Roadmap file should be removed like that I mean the file as is, not that we should not have  the concept of a roadmap.For github issues of course we need to group them either via label, milestone and/or in a project board like you show in order to to have a list of what is actually planned to be worked on.
I like the plan by @Luap99 - working with the issue tracker on this is easier than remembering to update the dedicated file for it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
